text:
Olympics



Russian figure skater Kamila Valieva, whose doping scandal threw the sport in turmoil at the 2022 Beijing Olympics, received a four-year ban from competition Monday from the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS).

The decision means Russia will be stripped of its gold medal in figure skating’s team event, and the U.S., which won silver, will become the Olympic champions. The International Olympic Committee did not present any medals in Beijing for the event after Valieva’s test results turned up positive for a banned heart medicine hours after she helped Russia win gold.

Advertisement

Japan won bronze and Canada placed fourth in the team event. The International Olympic Committee said at the time that it would “organize dignified medal ceremonies once the case of Ms. Valieva has been concluded.”

Valieva’s ban is retroactive to Dec. 25, 2021, and ends in 2025, making her eligible to compete in the 2026 Olympics. All of her competition results since Dec. 25, 2021, are disqualified, and she must forfeit any titles, awards, medals, profits, prizes and appearance money.

CAS said it upheld the World Anti-Doping Agency’s appeals after the agency asked the court to disqualify Valieva from theOlympics.

“Having carefully considered all the evidence put before it, the CAS panel concluded that Ms. Valieva was not able to establish, on the balance of probabilities and on the basis of the evidence before the Panel, that she had not committed the (doping violation) intentionally,” the court said in a statement.

Valieva, then 15 years old, tested positive for trimetazidine, and her lawyers have argued she was contaminated by traces of the medication used by her grandfather. Her sample was taken six weeks earlier at the Russian national championships but not flagged by a Swedish lab until hours after she skated in the Olympic team event on Feb. 7, 2022.

CAS then cleared Valieva to compete in the women’s competition despite her positive test and did not hold her responsible as a minor. Following that decision, the IOC said it would not be appropriate to hold the medal ceremony if Valieva made the podium in the women’s competition. However, shestruggled through her long program and failed to medal.

Jenna Westis a senior editor on The Athletic's news team. Before joining The Athletic, she served as a writer and producer for Sports Illustrated's national news desk. Jenna is a graduate of Northwestern University.
title: Russian figure skater Kamila Valieva given 4-year ban for doping, disqualified from 2022 Olympics - The Athletic
author: Jenna West
description: Valieva's positive test results for a banned heart medicine turned up hours after she helped Russia win gold in the team event.
